etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "tree") with a central dot -> core of the tree -> vermilion
etymology.interpretation: The character focuses on highlighting the core or center of a tree using an extra mark, indicating an idea of something central, core, or inner, akin to the essence or heart of the object, which metaphorically extends to the color vermilion associated with the energetic core.

examples.chinese: 他用朱笔在纸上写字(他用朱筆在紙上寫字)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng zhūbǐ zài zhǐ shàng xiězì.
examples.english: He wrote on the paper with a vermilion pen.

examples.chinese: 这幅画中充满了朱色和金色(這幅畫中充滿了朱色和金色)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè fú huà zhōng chōngmǎnle zhūsè hé jīnsè.
examples.english: The painting is full of vermilion and gold colors.

examples.chinese: 祭坛上放着一个朱色的箱子(祭壇上放著一個朱色的箱子)。
examples.pinyin: Jìtán shàng fàngzhe yīgè zhūsè de xiāngzi.
examples.english: There was a vermilion box placed on the altar.
